detail: 40 of 57 sampled government offices (70%) report NO internet connectivity despite listing under the County Connectivity Project; remaining 17 (30%) have unreliable NOFBI and use private ISPs; worst-affected: Civil Registration Services, Lands Registries, Police - offices where connectivity is a core service enabler; some areas have full infrastructure installed and no connectivity

NOFBI 2/2E is one backbone maintained under two government entities (State Department + ICT Authority) and multiple contracts (2 regional + 8 regional); duplication causes coordination failures and slow restoration; unmanaged cable found at Kisumu East DCC (July 2025 verification)

E-GP contract awarded 25 Apr 2022 to a consortium at USD 2,981,289 (KSh 344,794,744), 3 years to 25 Apr 2025; 45% paid (USD 1,352,969) by 14 Oct 2025; challenges: systemic, user, onboarding volume, supplier-registration backlog

finding: Health equipment fee-for-service without PPP approval
detail: Fixed fee-for-service medical equipment programme (7yr +3) implemented without PPP Committee approval (PPP Act 2021 ss.2, 59); 80% to 255% of revenue from 120 SHA-insured services paid out to suppliers - pay-out ratio above 100% for nine services, facilities compensate suppliers over SHA rates

Judiciary receiver of revenue: non-compliance with directive on e-Citizen integration and centralized revenue collection; State Law Office: failure to use the e-Citizen platform for marriage/registration fees
